title: Bacon Brings Couples Together on Flitch Day
story: Couples who haven't argued for a whole year can win a flitch of bacon on July 19! This quirky English tradition called National Flitch Day challenges married couples to prove they've been happy together without a single fight. If they can convince a jury of their marital bliss, they get rewarded with a side of bacon. Today, the famous ceremony still happens in Dunmow, England, where couples must kneel on stones and swear they've had zero arguments. It's one of the most delicious prizes for good behavior!

They can cook the best breakfast
They can dance while holding bacon
activity_answer: They haven't argued for a whole year
bonus_fact: The word "flitch" means a side of bacon cut from a pig.

story: Flames engulfed Rome's famous Circus Maximus on July 19, 64. This massive stadium, where chariot races thrilled crowds, became a fiery spectacle. The blaze spread quickly through the wooden seats and shops. Emperor Nero rushed to organize firefighters, but much of Rome burned. From the ashes, Romans rebuilt their city stronger than before.
activity_question: What type of events were held at the Circus Maximus?
bonus_fact: The Circus Maximus could hold about 150,000 spectators!

title: Beethoven's Beat Signals Victory
story: Dot-dot-dot-dash! In 1941, the BBC World Service started using a secret code. They played the opening notes of Beethoven's famous 5th Symphony. These four notes sound just like the letter V in Morse code. On July 19, listeners tuned in to hear this special signal, which stood for Victory. It was a clever way to boost spirits during tough times.
activity_question: What did the opening notes of Beethoven's 5th Symphony sound like in Morse code?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the BBC chose to use music as a secret code?

title: Churchill's Fingers Spark a Movement
story: Fingers raised high, Winston Churchill sparked a revolution. In 1941, Britain's leader launched the 'V for Victory' campaign. His simple hand gesture became a powerful symbol of hope. As war raged on July 19, Churchill's two-fingered salute united millions. Soon, people across Europe flashed the 'V' sign to show their spirit of resistance.
activity_question: What did Churchill's 'V' hand gesture represent?
bonus_fact: The 'V for Victory' symbol was also tapped out in Morse code, matching the rhythm of Beethoven's Fifth Symphony.

title: Elvis Shakes Up the Music World
story: A young singer named Elvis Presley burst onto the music scene in 1954. On July 19, his very first single hit the airwaves. It was a cover of Arthur Cruddup's song 'That's All Right'. Elvis's unique style blended country and rhythm and blues, creating a fresh sound that would soon take America by storm.
activity_question: What was the name of Elvis Presley's debut single?
bonus_fact: Elvis was only 19 years old when he released his first single.

title: Lewis Hamilton's Speedy Success in Hungary
story: Zoom! Lewis Hamilton raced into the history books on July 19. The British Formula 1 driver sped to victory at the Hungarian Grand Prix in Budapest in 2020. This win was extra special because it was Hamilton's eighth time winning this particular race. His amazing feat tied the record for most wins at a single circuit, matching racing legend Michael Schumacher's achievements in France.
activity_question: What record did Lewis Hamilton tie with his win in Hungary?
activity_choices: Most wins at a single circuit
activity_answer: Most wins at a single circuit
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous Formula 1 driver?

title: A Woman Makes History in Politics
story: Geraldine Ferraro shattered a glass ceiling in American politics. In 1984, she became the first woman chosen as a major party's vice-presidential nominee. The Democratic Party selected her to run alongside Walter Mondale in their bid for the White House. This groundbreaking moment on July 19 opened doors for women in high-level politics.
activity_question: What historic role did Geraldine Ferraro take on?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it took until 1984 for a woman to be nominated for vice president?

title: A Lightning-Fast Boxing Victory
story: Pow! In a flash, Tim Witherspoon claimed the heavyweight boxing title. On July 19, 1986, he knocked out Frank Bruno in just eleven rounds. The crowd roared as Witherspoon's powerful punches sent Bruno to the canvas. It was a thrilling match that boxing fans still talk about today.
activity_question: How many rounds did it take for Tim Witherspoon to win the match?
follow_up_question: What sport were Tim Witherspoon and Frank Bruno competing in?

title: Despacito Dances to Streaming Stardom
story: A catchy Latin beat took over the world in 2017. 'Despacito' by Luis Fonsi featuring Daddy Yankee became the most streamed song ever on July 19. The infectious tune had been played a staggering 4.6 billion times online. Its irresistible rhythm and Spanish lyrics captured hearts across the globe, proving music truly is a universal language.
activity_question: What song became the most streamed ever in 2017?
follow_up_question: Have you ever tried dancing to 'Despacito'? What's your favorite dance song?

title: Soaring to New Heights
story: Lynn Rippelmeyer made history in 1984 by becoming the first woman to captain a Boeing 747 across the Atlantic Ocean. On July 19, she took the controls of the massive jumbo jet, proving that the sky's the limit for female pilots. Her groundbreaking flight opened doors for more women to pursue careers in aviation and inspired girls to dream big.
activity_question: What type of aircraft did Lynn Rippelmeyer captain across the Atlantic?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for women to have opportunities in all career fields?

title: Movies Take Flight!
story: Passengers on a TWA flight in 1961 got a big surprise. For the first time ever, they could watch a movie while soaring through the clouds! On July 19, TWA showed the very first in-flight movie. Imagine settling into your seat and seeing a screen appear. It was like having a mini-theater right there in the airplane. This cool idea changed air travel forever, making long flights much more fun.
activity_question: What airline showed the first in-flight movie?
follow_up_question: What do you think was the biggest challenge in showing movies on planes back then?
